steps.title: Identify the appositive and determine if it's essential
steps.content:
”Plant cell biologist” is a title; “Yuree Lee” is a proper name identifying the specific person.

The sentence is about a discovery from a particular 2007 article—the author's name is needed to know WHO made it, not just added as extra info.

This makes the name essential—a restrictive appositive.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
Restrictive appositives take no commas; non-restrictive appositives take commas on both sides (mid-sentence).

Choice C uses no commas, correctly treating the name as essential identification.

Choices A and B have only one comma each (unbalanced and incorrect); Choice D uses both commas, treating the name as parenthetical extra info, but the name is needed to identify the author.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
